comparison_basis: Runtime-informed cross-site launch planning support example. Not a claim of plant truth.

station_gap_delta_sec.note: Laser / blanking flow shows more restart and setup loss at the mirrored launch site.
Forming / bending standard work remains less stable in the Mexico ramp-up example.
Feature preparation and handling are more exposed to launch rework at the overseas site.
Dimensional inspection remains the main shared bottleneck, with worse launch loss in Mexico.
Packaging / handoff / traceability burden is materially higher in the Mexico example.

key_differences: Mexico example has lower average FPY and higher average downtime.
Mexico example shows one additional station above target CT.
Inspection and packaging gaps are materially larger in the Mexico example.
recommended_actions: Tighten launch layered audits at ST30, ST60, and ST70 before relaxing staffing assumptions.
Mirror proven Korea standard work and issue-response logic into the Mexico ramp-up package.
Keep the comparison framed as runtime-informed early production-engineering support, not plant truth.
